Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks bills (via quickbooks_search_bills) for Heartland Movers to find the Q1 bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each), the HOLD version HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD (with lines 6-8 marked DISPUTED: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift), and the Q4 2025 bill HM-2025-Q4-0031 ($6,400, clean). Discover that the original bill still carries the full $12,800 balance while the hold version annotates the disputed lines but does not reduce the total.
OE 2: Search emails for Heartland Movers correspondence to find Marcus's formal dispute letter to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com (accepting 5 moves at $8,000, disputing 3 at $4,800), Chloe's review findings forwarded to Marcus and Hana, and Jake Loomis's formal rate increase notice (20% across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, tied directly to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute, CC'd to chloe.vance@moveops.com).
OE 3: Search Slack in #finance (C005) and #operations (C006) for Heartland reconciliation discussions to find Marcus's payment hold note, Hana's expanded April 14 review finding that 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves have billing discrepancies beyond the original dispute: (1) Kevin Tran file includes $650 in unauthorized storage charges, (2) Priya Nair file includes an extra shuttle fee that does not match origin access notes, and (3) the original Marchetti/Osei/Venkatesh dispute at $4,800. Discover the VRECON-2026-Q1-HM tracking reference.
OE 4: Search Airtable relocation records (via airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) for employees listed on the Heartland Q1 invoice to cross-check: valid moves (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves = 5 at $1,600 each), cancelled (Marchetti, Osei), and misattributed (Venkatesh was handled by Swift Relocations, not Heartland).
OE 5: Search Linear issues (via linear_list_issues) for Heartland and vendor-related tracking tickets: issue_heartland_q1_recon (OPS-342, Vendor invoice reconciliation — Heartland Movers Q1 2026, assigned to Chloe), linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response, assigned to Marcus), linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM, assigned to Alejandro), and linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options, assigned to Marcus).
OE 6: Search emails for Swift Relocations correspondence to find Greg Pallone's immediate rate increase notice to Chloe (15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings + $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, effective immediately as of April 11). Also find Greg's earlier volume pricing proposal from March with the 12%-18% corporate discount tiers.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks bills for Swift Relocations to find historical bills: bill_swift_tran (Kevin Tran, Portland to Denver, $2,885), bill_swift_venkatesh (Priya Venkatesh, Houston to Seattle, $4,450), bill_swift_kovac (Ines Kovac, Brooklyn to Austin, $3,675). These establish the pre-increase cost basis for quantifying margin impact under the new rate structure.
OE 8: Search Slack #finance and email for Alejandro's margin exposure analysis. Find his Slack message noting the data problem: unable to produce a defensible client-by-client margin forecast because inputs live in three disconnected systems (Airtable relocations, QuickBooks bills, CRM deals). Determine that his model is directional only, not a complete forecast, and needs the reconciliation layer.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for the Mosaic Robotics contingent liability accrual: bill ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000, against acct_contingent_liability_claims, vendor_heartland, marked DO NOT PAY). Discover the $50,000 vendor coverage cap per item and $40,000 MoveOps direct exposure. Cross-reference credit memo CM-2026-0415 and invoice INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 for 3 relocations).
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for the DOT penalty accrual: bill ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000, expense line against acct_regulatory_compliance_expense, with the corresponding liability tracked on acct_dot_penalty_accrual, for Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187). Based on one confirmed documentation gap: missing placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment. Approved by Marcus Thorne.
OE 11: Cross-check both accrual amounts against operational updates. Search emails and Slack for Mosaic prototype damage developments (insurance claim status, Emeka's formal incident report to Tamara Byrd, Chloe's incident brief), and DOT compliance developments (extension granted, Blessing's missing documentation, hazmat training scheduled). Search CRM engagement notes for engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 with the full damage chain-of-custody timeline.
OE 12: Search CRM for vendor company records (Heartland Movers, Swift Relocations, Lakeline Logistics) and client deals to understand the relationship context and identify which active client accounts route through Swift versus Heartland (e.g., GreenStack, NorthWind, Vectral, PivotPoint).
OE 13: Look up email addresses for Elena Rostova (elena.rostova@moveops.com), Hana Kim (hana.kim@moveops.com), Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com), and Alejandro Fuentes (alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts.
OE 14: Send email (via send_email) from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC hana.kim@moveops.com, with a consolidated summary covering: (1) Heartland Q1 dispute status with net payable and disputed amounts plus the 20% Q3 rate increase, (2) Swift immediate 15% rate increase and $500 DOT surcharge with margin impact analysis, (3) Mosaic $90,000 accrual status with coverage gap assessment, (4) DOT $50,000 penalty accrual status — each with supporting evidence.
OE 15: Post a message to #finance (C005) (via conversations_add_message) with the key financial figures covering all three vendor positions and both liability accruals so Alejandro has visibility to incorporate into his margin model.
OE 16: Add comments to existing Linear tracking tickets (via linear_create_comment) with updated analysis findings. Relevant tickets include: issue_heartland_q1_recon, linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (broader billing discrepancies), linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (Q3 margin dataset), and/or linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (CEO vendor strategy memo).
OE 17: Create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for Thursday April 30, 2026 (afternoon), 30 minutes, titled "vendor strategy review" (or similar), with chloe.vance@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com as attendees.
OE 18: Correct the Heartland vendor payable in QuickBooks (via quickbooks_create_bill or quickbooks_update_account) to reflect the supported amount of approximately $8,000 for the 5 valid moves, rather than the full $12,800 that the original bill still carries. Alternatively, if QuickBooks tools cannot directly modify the existing bill, the agent documents in the final response and email that the $12,800 entry overstates the liability and the recognized payable should be $8,000.
OE 19: Assess whether the Mosaic $90,000 contingent-liability accrual and the DOT $50,000 penalty accrual are current or stale by cross-referencing operational updates from OE 11. Either adjust the accrual amounts in QuickBooks (via quickbooks_create_bill or quickbooks_update_account) if developments warrant a change, or provide a clear written explanation in the final response and email to Elena documenting why each current figure remains defensible.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
